qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Three bypass paths in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py: BUG1 combined -c flags (bash -lc), BUG2 sudo option-arg pairs (sudo -u root), BUG3 absolute-path shell in heredoc pipe (cat <<EOF | /bin/bash).
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 3 bypass paths confirmed fixed: (1) BUG1 line 323 — compound predicate 'c in t[1:]' now catches -lc, -ce, +c forms; (2) BUG2 lines 224-244 — _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG + inner loop correctly skips sudo option-arg pairs; (3) BUG3 lines 378-380 — after.split('|')[1:] + _first_word()/_SHELL_INTERPS correctly handles absolute-path shells. All 40 hook tests pass; all 10 generated tests pass.

complaint_element_pointer.page_url: N/A — library module, no browser UI

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. Iteration 2 fixes both issues that caused QA-iter1 FAIL: (1) BA-A-iter2 added re.MULTILINE to AC7 verification_command — confirmed present; (2) Dev-A-stubs filled all 10 TEST_INCOMPLETE stubs in tests/generated/20260524-125300-A/ — confirmed 10 passed with no sentinel in source files. All 40 hook tests pass. All 10 generated tests pass. Provenance confirmed. Codex adversarial review surfaced out-of-scope observations (manifest stale verification commands, AC8 negative-assertion blind spot) — non-blocking.

root_cause_verification.rationale: All three bypass paths are fixed. BUG1: line 323 of bash_context_strip.py now uses compound predicate '(t.startswith("-") or t.startswith("+")) and not t.startswith("--") and "c" in t[1:]' replacing exact equality. BUG2: _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG frozenset at module level, inner loop in _find_command_word consuming option-arg pairs after sudo detection. BUG3: shell_ctx computation now uses any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split("|")[1:]) replacing the removed regex. git diff from baseline f79fba1c confirms all changes are present.

build_verification.skip_reason: Pure Python library — no build or deploy step. Module is imported directly. Verified by running python3 -c import statements and pytest.

provenance_check.detail: Both files listed in dev.files_modified appear in git diff --name-only output from baseline SHA. No provenance violation.

regression_test_results.details: 40 passed in 0.04s. All 30 pre-existing tests pass (no regressions). All 10 new tests pass.

band_aid_scan.detail: The BUG1 fix tightens detection (now catches more combined-flag forms). The BUG2 fix adds option-arg pair consumption logic. The BUG3 fix replaces a regex with a more precise membership-based check that both fixes a false-positive and adds capability. No weakening of any existing check detected. The removed regex is replaced by a stricter formulation, not loosened.

It has no HTTP interface, no frontend, and no user-visible UI surface. The user requirement is to fix three bypass paths in a hook library. Playwright testing is not applicable.

manifest_verification.detail: pytest tests/generated/20260524-125300-A/ -v: 10 passed in 0.31s. grep -l TEST_INCOMPLETE *.py returned empty. .pyc hits are stale bytecode from prior stub state (pre-iter2), irrelevant. Dev-A-stubs filled all 10 test bodies with real assertions. Manifest hook_check.verification_command for AC7/AC8 are stale (lack re.MULTILINE) but actual test files use grep_match() which is line-by-line and correct — surfaced as out_of_scope_observations.

generated_tests.description: AC3: sudo -u root shifts to bash; rm exposed
AC4: sudo -H no-arg flag; rm exposed
AC5: sudo -- terminates options; rm exposed
AC6: heredoc piped to /bin/bash preserves body
AC7: heredoc piped to /usr/bin/bash preserves body; kill visible
AC8: non-shell /tmp/bash.old in pipe does NOT preserve body
AC9: full test suite >= 30 tests pass

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Manifest hook_check.verification_command for AC7 is stale (pre-iter2, lacks re.MULTILINE). Without re.MULTILINE, the manifest AC7 command would FAIL even on a correct implementation (kill on line 2 preceded by \n not in char class). Actual test file uses grep_match() and passes correctly. Dimension: spec_text_vs_execution_drift (manifest vs test-file vs acceptance-criteria). Non-blocking: actual test file correct.

AC8 verification_command uses re.search(pattern, out) WITHOUT re.MULTILINE for a NEGATIVE assertion. If the implementation were broken (body not stripped), rm on line 2 preceded by \n would not be caught (^ fails without MULTILINE). The implementation is currently correct so not a live bug, but the command cannot detect the regression it was designed to catch. Codex PROPOSED_FIX: add re.MULTILINE to AC8 verification_command. Non-blocking since implementation IS correct. Dimension: spec_text_vs_execution_drift.

BUG3 fix uses raw after.split('|') without respecting shell quoting. A line like "cat <<'EOF' | tee 'a|b' | /bin/bash" would split into three segments, treating 'a|b' as a split point. Same precision class as original bugs. Per BA spec C1 deferral class. OBSERVATION_ONLY — no PROPOSED_FIX in scope.

doas and run0 are in _WRAPPER_PREFIXES but lack option-argument pair skipping analogous to BUG2 fix for sudo. 'doas -u root bash -c ...' produces same bypass class. Per BA spec C1 deferral class (listed in dev.recommendations).

self_verification.notes: All 10 AC verification_commands executed directly with captured output. pytest run directly for both suites. git diff --name-only captured for provenance. TEST_INCOMPLETE grep run on source .py files (no hits). re.MULTILINE presence checked via Python json parse. Codex adversarial consultation completed with full output captured.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex surfaced one finding with PROPOSED_FIX: AC8 verification_command uses re.search without re.MULTILINE for a negative assertion — false-pass risk in regression case. Codex also flagged manifest.json AC7/AC8 as stale. Both are BA-spec/manifest metadata issues, not implementation issues. Actual test files use grep_match() (line-by-line) which is correct. Codex confirmed implementation is sound.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both codex findings added to out_of_scope_observations with spec_text_vs_execution_drift dimension. Not added to failures[] — actual tests pass correctly, implementation is correct. Verdict remains PASS.

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 2 verification: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py and hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py verified against baseline SHA f79fba1c1684d246928a3cfb39bf1ed7a9a5c8a5. Full hook test suite: 40 passed (30 original + 10 new). Generated test suite tests/generated/20260524-125300-A/: 10 passed, zero TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels in source .py files. AC7 verification_command confirmed to contain re.MULTILINE (BA-A-iter2 fix). All 10 AC verification_commands executed directly — all pass. BUG1 fix: line 323, compound predicate. BUG2 fix: lines 224-244, _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG + inner loop. BUG3 fix: lines 378-380, any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:]).

summary.out_of_scope_note: 4 observations (manifest stale AC7/AC8, AC8 negative-assertion MULTILINE blind spot, BUG3 pipe-split quote-awareness gap, doas/run0 arity table gap) — all non-blocking, none in user path for current cycle scope.
